nodes.x: Fret not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.
Fret not thyself because of evil men, neither be thou envious at the wicked;
The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.
Yea, even wo unto all ye workers of iniquity; repent, repent, for the Lord God hath spoken it!
Be not thou envious against evil men, neither desire to be with them.
Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.
Whilst it is yet in his greenness, and not cut down, it withereth before any other herb.
Let them be as the grass upon the housetops, which withereth afore it groweth up:

Yea, they shall not be planted; yea, they shall not be sown: yea, their stock shall not take root in the earth: and he shall also blow upon them, and they shall wither, and the whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.

green herb for meat, and all these things shall be thu9 organized. And the Gods said we will do every thing that we have said, and organize them; and, behold, they shall be very obedient. And it came to pass that it was from evening until morning, they called night; and it come to pass that it was from morning until evening, they called day; and they numbered the sixth time.
For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
Trust in the Lord, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
And thou shalt do that which is right and good in the sight of the Lord: that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest go in and possess the good land which the Lord sware unto thy fathers,
And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.
That which is altogether just shalt thou follow, that thou mayest live, and inherit the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
Be thou prepared, and prepare for thyself, thou, and all thy company that are assembled unto thee, and be thou a guard unto them.
Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thine heart:
Grant thee according to thine own heart, and fulfil all thy counsel.
And it shall come to pass in the day that the Lord shall give thee rest from thy sorrow, and from thy fear, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serve,
And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.
And it shall come to pass in that day that the Lord shall give thee rest, from thy sorrow, and from thy fear, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serve.
Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy thoughts shall be established.
Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the Lord.
Trust in the Lord, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.

Go trusting in God, and continue to trust in him, and he will open
your way and multiply blessings upon you, and your souls will be
satisfied with his goodness. I cannot promise you any good in taking an
unrighteous course; your lives must be examples of good works. 8:73.

Keep the commandments of the Lord; observe the instructions and counsel you receive from those placed over you to preside; be faithful and industrious, economienl and prudent; sock continually unto the Lord for wisdom, and train up your children in His nurture and admonition, that when wo shall have finished our pilgrimage upon the earth, we may go hence in peace, having wrought; righteousness…
And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
And he shall rise the third day from the dead; and behold, he standeth to judge the world; and behold, all these things are done that a righteous judgment might come upon the children of men.
And he who is faithful shall overcome all things, and shall be lifted up at the last day.

And he shall come into the world to redeem his people; and he shall take upon him the transgressions of those who believe on his name; and these are they that shall have eternal life, and salvation cometh to none else.
And he that liveth in righteousness shall be changed in the twinkling of an eye, and the earth shall pass away so as by fire.
He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.
Rest in the Lord, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.
Save thyself, and come down from the cross.
Wait on the Lord: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the Lord.
Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.
¶ Go thy way, eat thy bread with joy, and drink thy wine with a merry heart; for God now accepteth thy works.
Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The Lord be magnified.
Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
¶ Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the Lord, and depart from evil.
Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the earth.
For such as be blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and they that be cursed of him shall be cut off.
And the earth shall be given unto them for an inheritance; and they shall multiply and wax strong, and their children shall grow up without sin unto salvation.
And death shall be chosen rather than life by all the residue of them that remain of this evil family, which remain in all the places whither I have driven them, saith the Lord of hosts.
For yet a little while, and the wicked shall not be: yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it shall not be.
Again, when I say unto the wicked, Thou shalt surely die; if he turn from his sin, and do that which is lawful and right;
But if the wicked turn from his wickedness, and do that which is lawful and right, he shall live thereby.

That be far from thee to do after this manner, to slay the righteous with the wicked: and that the righteous should be as the wicked, that be far from thee: Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?
But the meek shall inherit the earth; and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
And blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
And the meek also shall increase, and their joy shall be in the Lord, and the poor among men shall rejoice in the Holy One of Israel.
The wicked plotteth against the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.
The wicked shall see it, and be grieved; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.
He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.
And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.
The wicked is snared by the transgression of his lips: but the just shall come out of trouble.
The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
And it shall come to pass that he that feareth me shall be looking forth for the great day of the Lord to come, even for the signs of the coming of the Son of Man.

And behold, this will I give unto you for a sign at the time of his coming; for behold, there shall be great lights in heaven, insomuch that in the night before he cometh there shall be no darkness, insomuch that it shall appear unto man as if it was day.
The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, and to slay such as be of upright conversation.
For, lo, the wicked bend their bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may privily shoot at the upright in heart.
A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.
Who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the frowardness of the wicked;
¶ That they may do evil with both hands earnestly, the prince asketh, and the judge asketh for a reward; and the great man, he uttereth his mischievous desire: so they wrap it up.
Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
Their bows shall also dash the young men to pieces; and they shall have no pity on the fruit of the womb; their eyes shall not spare children.
Their bows also shall dash the young men to pieces; and they shall have no pity on the fruit of the womb; their eye shall not spare children.
They bow themselves, they bring forth their young ones, they cast out their sorrows.
They shall fall in the midst of them that are slain by the sword: she is delivered to the sword: draw her and all her multitudes.

And their arm shall be my arm, and I will be their shield and their buckler; and I will gird up their loins, and they shall fight manfully for me; and their enemies shall be under their feet; and I will let fall the sword in their behalf, and by the fire of mine indignation will I preserve them.
A little that a righteous man hath is better than the riches of many wicked.
Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death.
In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble.
Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
By humility and the fear of the Lord are riches, and honour, and life.
For the arms of the wicked shall be broken: but the Lord upholdeth the righteous.
Break thou the arm of the wicked and the evil man: seek out his wickedness till thou find none.
The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.
And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.
Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end; but establish the just: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reins.
Though hand join in hand, the wicked shall not be unpunished: but the seed of the righteous shall be delivered.
The Lord knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.
Therefore shall they have no inheritance among their brethren: the Lord is their inheritance, as he hath said unto them.

For those that live shall inherit the earth, and those that die shall rest from all their labors, and their works shall follow them; and they shall receive a crown in the mansions of my Father, which I have prepared for them.

everlasting inheritance upon this earth, in its glorified and immortal state. Therefore how careful the Latter-day Saints should be in order to merit the association of the happy throng whom John heard singing that new song. We desire our inheritance on this earth as well as they. If they could rejoice in anticipation of receiving an inheritance on the earth, how much more can we who know…
They shall not be ashamed in the evil time: and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
For they shall eat, and not have enough: they shall commit whoredom, and shall not increase: because they have left off to take heed to the Lord.

When I shall send upon them the evil arrows of famine, which shall be for their destruction, and which I will send to destroy you: and I will increase the famine upon you, and will break your staff of bread:
And ye shall eat in plenty, and be satisfied, and praise the name of the Lord your God, that hath dealt wondrously with you: and my people shall never be ashamed.
They shall also gird themselves with sackcloth, and horror shall cover them; and shame shall be upon all faces, and baldness upon all their heads.

XVII.  And  the  expression,  "  they  were  not  ashamed,"  we 
will  examine  hereafter  :  for  there  ai'e  three  ideas  brought  for- 
ward in  this  passage.  Shamelessness,  modesty,  and  a  state  of 
indifference,  in  which  one  is  neither  shameless  nor  modest. 
Now  shamelessness  is  the  property  of  a  worthless  person,  and 
modesty  the  characteristic  of  a  virtuous …
But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the Lord shall be as the fat of lambs: they shall consume; into smoke shall they consume away.
Then shall the lambs feed after their manner, and the waste places of the fat ones shall strangers eat.
For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord, even the soul that eateth it shall be cut off from his people.
And ye shall perish among the heathen, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.

And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the Lord; the fat thereof, and the whole rump, it shall he take off hard by the backbone; and the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the Lord; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
The wicked borroweth, and payeth not again: but the righteous sheweth mercy, and giveth.
For behold, justice exerciseth all his demands, and also mercy claimeth all which is her own; and thus, none but the truly penitent are saved.
Shew us thy mercy, O Lord, and grant us thy salvation.
Mercy unto you, and peace, and love, be multiplied.
He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.
For such as be blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and they that be cursed of him shall be cut off.

That whoever he blesses shall be blessed, and whoever he curses shall be cursed; that whatsoever he shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven; and whatsoever he shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the earth.
And he shall turn the heart of the fathers to the children, and the heart of the children to their fathers, lest I come and smite the earth with a curse.
Nevertheless, he that endureth in faith and doeth my will, the same shall overcome, and shall receive an inheritance upon the earth when the day of transfiguration shall come;
The steps of a good man are ordered by the Lord: and he delighteth in his way.
¶ O Lord, I know that the way of man is not in himself: it is not in man that walketh to direct his steps.
A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.
Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
For the ways of man are before the eyes of the Lord, and he pondereth all his goings.
Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the Lord upholdeth him with his hand.
For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.
For when he dieth he shall carry nothing away: his glory shall not descend after him.
Thou hast made his glory to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground.
The Lord upholdeth all that fall, and raiseth up all those that be bowed down.
The Lord will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
I have been young, and now am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.
And if he will do this I will bless him with a multiplicity of blessings, that he shall not be forsaken, nor his seed be found begging bread.

righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.” The seed of the righteous will never be found begging bread; for the Lord will provide for his people in the latter days. He has defended us so far, and has fought our battles, has led us to victory, and blessed us with houses and lands, with friends, and with an abundance of the comforts of life. We are filled with peace, joy and consolation. We…
Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;)
He is ever merciful, and lendeth; and his seed is blessed.
And now, behold, are ye sure that this is a good seed? I say unto you, Yea; for every seed bringeth forth unto its own likeness.
Neither shall he profane his seed among his people: for I the Lord do sanctify him.
And this is the beginning of the stewardship which I have appointed unto him, for a blessing unto him and his seed after him.
He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.
Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
Abstain from all appearance of evil.
Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
¶ Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil men.
For the Lord loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.
The Lord preserveth all them that love him: but all the wicked will he destroy.
He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.
But the things of the wise and the prudent shall be hid from them forever—yea, that happiness which is prepared for the saints.
The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
Thy people also shall be all righteous: they shall inherit the land for ever, the branch of my planting, the work of my hands, that I may be glorified.
And the time speedily cometh, that except ye repent they shall possess the land of your inheritance, and the Lord God will lead away the righteous out from among you.
And then shall the righteous shine forth in the kingdom of God.
And it shall be, when thou art come in unto the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee for an inheritance, and possessest it, and dwellest therein;
The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.
The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips.
My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out.
Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.

He that walketh righteously, and speaketh uprightly; he that despiseth the gain of oppressions, that shaketh his hands from holding of bribes, that stoppeth his ears from hearing of blood, and shutteth his eyes from seeing evil;
The law of his God is in his heart; none of his steps shall slide.
Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.
This is the law of him that hath an issue, and of him whose seed goeth from him, and is defiled therewith;
This is eternal lives—to know the only wise and true God, and Jesus Christ, whom he hath sent. I am he. Receive ye, therefore, my law.
The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.
The Lord says the wicked shall slay the wicked, and he orders it so. I pray God that he will preserve the righteous, and endue his people who
Then hear thou in heaven, and do, and judge thy servants, condemning the wicked, to bring his way upon his head; and justifying the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.

Then hear thou from heaven, and do, and judge thy servants, by requiting the wicked, by recompensing his way upon his own head; and by justifying the righteous, by giving him according to his righteousness.
The Lord will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
For the Lord will judge his people, and he will repent himself concerning his servants.

at some future time. All the warnings that have been given to the Latter-day Saints, and to the world, will again come up, in the own due time of the Lord, in judgment; and it will be required of us to render an account, whether we have been obedient to those warnings, or whether we have been disobedient. The Lord is a consistent Being in all his doings. He will not condemn the children of men,…
For the Lord shall judge his people, and repent himself for his servants, when he seeth that their power is gone, and there is none shut up, or left.
Wait on the Lord, and keep his way, and he shall exalt thee to inherit the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see it.
The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
By little and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.

Speak not thou in thine heart, after that the Lord thy God hath cast them out from before thee, saying, For my righteousness the Lord hath brought me in to possess this land: but for the wickedness of these nations the Lord doth drive them out from before thee.

Not for thy righteousness, or for the uprightness of thine heart, dost thou go to possess their land: but for the wickedness of these nations the Lord thy God doth drive them out from before thee, and that he may perform the word which the Lord sware unto thy fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading himself like a green bay tree.
Wherefore do the wicked live, become old, yea, are mighty in power?
Yea, and we also see the great wickedness one very wicked man can cause to take place among the children of men.
Yet he filled their houses with good things: but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
For I was envious at the foolish, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Yet he passed away, and, lo, he was not: yea, I sought him, but he could not be found.
By night on my bed I sought him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.
I opened to my beloved; but my beloved had withdrawn himself, and was gone: my soul failed when he spake: I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.

Search was made for the watchman, but he had disappeared. Awaking to a
consciousness of what mischief he had caused through his carelessness,
he had slipped away in the confusion, and was not likely to return.
But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me.
Mark the perfect man, and behold the upright: for the end of that man is peace.
For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it.
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.
That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.
But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.
But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it.
¶ And the destruction of the transgressors and of the sinners shall be together, and they that forsake the Lord shall be consumed.
And thus cometh the end of the wicked, according to the prophecy of Moses, saying: They shall be cut off from among the people; but the end of the earth is not yet, but by and by.
Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?
But the salvation of the righteous is of the Lord: he is their strength in the time of trouble.
The Lord is righteous in all his ways, and holy in all his works.
But wo, wo unto him who knoweth that he rebelleth against God! For salvation cometh to none such except it be through repentance and faith on the Lord Jesus Christ.
For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
But of him are ye in Christ Jesus, who of God is made unto us wisdom, and righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption:
But he that remaineth steadfast and is not overcome, the same shall be saved.
And the Lord shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.
And they shall look to the poor and the needy, and administer to their relief that they shall not suffer; and send them forth to the place which I have commanded them;
And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the Lord our God shall cut them off.
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
And the priest shall bring them before the Lord, and shall offer his sin offering, and his burnt offering:
Then they cry unto the Lord in their trouble, and he saveth them out of their distresses.
